verses.text: Josiah becomes king at eight and does right, walking in David's ways without turning aside. A child set early toward God grows into a faithful king. Grace can take root young and bear a lifetime of obedience.

Josiah becomes king at eight and does right, walking in David's ways without turning aside. A child set early toward God grows into a faithful king. Grace can take root young and bear a lifetime of obedience.

Josiah orders the temple repaired, entrusting the money to faithful workmen who need no accounting. The neglected house of God is restored, and honest hands handle the funds. Renewal often begins with simply caring for what was let fall into disrepair.

Josiah orders the temple repaired, entrusting the money to faithful workmen who need no accounting. The neglected house of God is restored, and honest hands handle the funds. Renewal often begins with simply caring for what was let fall into disrepair.

During the repairs, Hilkiah finds the book of the law, long lost, and it is read before the king. The forgotten Scripture comes to light. A people can drift far while God's own word lies buried and unread in His house.

During the repairs, Hilkiah finds the book of the law, long lost, and it is read before the king. The forgotten Scripture comes to light. A people can drift far while God's own word lies buried and unread in His house.

During the repairs, Hilkiah finds the book of the law, long lost, and it is read before the king. The forgotten Scripture comes to light. A people can drift far while God's own word lies buried and unread in His house.

Hearing the law, Josiah tears his clothes, realizing how far the nation has fallen, and sends to inquire of the LORD. The word convicts and humbles him at once. Scripture truly heard does not flatter but pierces, driving the honest heart to repentance.

The prophetess Huldah confirms that God will bring on this place all the curses of the book, because they forsook Him. The judgment is just and sure. God's written warnings are not idle; what He recorded long ago He will surely perform.

The prophetess Huldah confirms that God will bring on this place all the curses of the book, because they forsook Him. The judgment is just and sure. God's written warnings are not idle; what He recorded long ago He will surely perform.

But because Josiah's heart was tender and he humbled himself and wept, God will gather him to his grave in peace before the disaster comes. Genuine humbling moves God to mercy. He marks the contrite heart and deals with it gently, even amid coming judgment.
